mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate over a 3-month rolling window signals labor market tightening and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased equity valuations through higher expected earnings growth and lower equity risk premiums over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.1% as of Sept 2011 indicates slack labor market
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with gradual labor market healing
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following UNRATE declines do not exceed baseline returns by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels (0.08%) constrains further monetary accommodation, signaling an exhausted easing cycle. Equity markets respond negatively as the marginal stimulus effect diminishes and tail risks from policy uncertainty increase over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 2+ consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC reverses sign and remains negative for 3+ consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer spending. This dual deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward across cyclical sectors.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and HOUST decline > 3% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 571k units and RSAFS at $395.5B as of Aug 2011
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between RSAFS and HOUST is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 6-month trend, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and pricing power. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit margins expand and inflation expectations remain anchored.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds its 6-month moving average by > 0.5 percentage points
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO acceleration are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 50k jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains elevated above 8.5%, signaling a stalled labor market recovery. This dual weakness predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession risk premia increase.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change < 50k AND UNRATE > 8.5% for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131.3M and UNRATE at 9.1% as of Sept 2011
regime_hint: double-dip recession risk with labor market stagnation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE deterioration does not produce a statistically significant negative coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates below 2% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains at zero, signaling deflationary pressure and policy impotence. Equity markets respond negatively over 20 trading days as real discount rates rise and growth expectations contract.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 226.27 and FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of Aug-Sept 2011
regime_hint: deflation risk with exhausted monetary policy
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 58% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term between low inflation and zero rates is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling an economy approaching stall-speed. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations are revised downward and equity risk premiums expand.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth remains positive and above its 12-month trend while inflation remains subdued (CPIAUCSL < 2.5% YoY), signaling healthy supply expansion without cost-push pressures. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as profit margins are protected and growth remains intact.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 94.04 and CPIAUCSL at 226.27 as of Aug 2011
regime_hint: goldilocks supply regime with stable inflation
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between INDPRO growth and low inflation is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
